mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25% and remains there for at least 2 consecutive months, the zero lower bound constraint becomes binding. This signals exhausted conventional monetary policy transmission, reducing the equity risk premium compression channel and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors reassess growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.15% as of April 2015 indicates ZLB regime
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window during ZLB periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth decelerates below 100,000 persons while unemployment rate remains above 9.5%, signaling labor market slack and weak aggregate demand. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings growth expectations contract.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.8% and PAYEMS at 130,947k as of March 2015
regime_hint: post-crisis labor market recovery with persistent slack
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the joint signal (both conditions met) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP growth below 2.0% annualized rate, when lagged 6 months, predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as the market reprices earnings forecasts downward in response to weakening macroeconomic momentum.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances when GDPC1 growth < 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declines month-over-month while remaining below its 12-month moving average, indicating supply-side weakness and margin compression. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors immediately reprice profit expectations.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO monthly change < 0% AND INDPRO < 12-month MA
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 70% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month while housing starts (HOUST) simultaneously rise above 600k annualized, indicating synchronized consumer and residential demand strength. This joint signal produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings growth accelerates.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth > 0.5% AND HOUST > 600k annualized
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 344,688M and HOUST at 598k as of February 2015
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across consumer and housing sectors
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) decelerates below 1.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains above 0.10%, creating a positive real rate environment. This signals monetary tightening in real terms and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as discount rates rise.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 215.79 and FEDFUNDS at 0.15% as of March 2015
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal shows Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate declines by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month rolling window, signaling accelerating labor market tightening. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as wage growth expectations rise and corporate earnings are repriced upward.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when UNRATE declines > 0.3pp, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) fall below 550k annualized while real retail sales (RSAFS) simultaneously decline month-over-month, indicating synchronized contraction in consumer and residential demand. This joint signal produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as forward earnings estimates are immediately revised downward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 550k annualized AND RSAFS MoM change < 0%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 598k and RSAFS at 344,688M as of February 2015
regime_hint: synchronized demand contraction across consumer and housing
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
